name: Piezoelectric Vibration Sensor
purpose: Detects mechanical vibrations from throat or chest surface to capture speech-related vibrations and articulatory movements.
role: Captures vibration patterns during phoneme production. The RMS and spectral features help differentiate between voiced and unvoiced phonemes, aiding in phoneme prediction and speech reconstruction.

name: MEMS Microphone (ICS-43434)
purpose: High-fidelity digital microphone for capturing acoustic signals and ambient sound with high SNR (Signal-to-Noise Ratio).
role: Primary audio sensor for phoneme recognition. MFCC features are extracted for machine learning-based speech classification. Works alongside the vibration sensor for multimodal phoneme prediction.

name: Airflow Sensor (FS3000)
purpose: Measures airflow velocity near the mouth or nose to track breathing patterns and speech-related airflow changes.
role: Detects speech onset by monitoring airflow bursts. Airflow exceeding a threshold indicates speech activity. Used for voice activity detection (VAD) and distinguishing plosive vs. fricative phonemes.

name: Pressure Sensor (XGZP6897D)
purpose: Measures differential air pressure to detect subtle pressure changes during speech, swallowing, and respiratory events.
role: Complements the airflow sensor by providing pressure data during speech events. Pressure patterns correlate with specific consonant and vowel articulations, improving phoneme classification accuracy.

name: IMU (LSM6DSO)
purpose: 6-axis Inertial Measurement Unit (accelerometer + gyroscope) for tracking head, jaw, and neck movements during speech.
role: Captures articulatory gestures and head movements correlated with speech patterns. IMU data helps normalize other sensor readings against motion artifacts and provides additional features for phoneme prediction models.

name: Temperature & Humidity (ASAIR ATH25)
purpose: Monitors ambient temperature and humidity levels for environmental context and sensor calibration purposes.
role: Provides environmental normalization data. Temperature and humidity affect sound propagation and sensor sensitivity. These values are used to calibrate and normalize readings from other sensors for consistent performance across varying conditions.
